Title: A conserved fungal glycosyltransferase facilitates pathogenesis of plants by enabling hyphal growth on solid surfaces

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: fungal extracellular/outer cell wall polysaccharide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: outer cell wall / extracellular matrix polysaccharide module
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely synthesizes or modifies an unknown extracellular or outer cell wall polysaccharide required for hyphal growth on solid surfaces
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms or modifies the functional outer wall/extracellular polysaccharide required for contact-dependent hyphal extension
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: unknown extracellular or outer cell wall polysaccharide
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fungal outer cell wall / extracellular matrix
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports hyphal extension on solid surfaces, likely by maintaining outer wall integrity and reducing friction/shear during tip growth; required for plant pathogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: cell wall fraction / outer cell wall-associated
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: increased under conditions favoring hyphal growth, including elevated temperature, low nutrients, and early growth on wheat leaf surfaces (48 hpi)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: relatively low in YPD, PDB and CDB broth at 16°C; increased in YPD at 25°C, in liquid water, and highest on wheat leaf surfaces at 48 hpi
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: temperature shift to 25°C; nutrient limitation; growth on solid surfaces; wheat leaf surface infection
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for contact-dependent hyphal growth on solid surfaces; not established as biochemical rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential for normal hyphal growth on solid surfaces and virulence; deletion causes short sinusoidal hyphae, outer wall abnormalities, and complete loss of pathogenicity on wheat leaves while yeast-like and liquid hyphal growth remain largely unaffected
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes severe virulence defect and contact-growth impairment; constitutive overexpression of several secreted and transmembrane proteins including Zt3LysM and a putative alpha-1,3-glucan synthase
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (whole-genome expression profiling; no accession reported in text); qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Global transcriptomics indicated surface sensing and secretion responses were largely retained in mutants; monosaccharide profiling did not detect depletion of major wall sugars, suggesting the product is a minor or masked polysaccharide
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. T-DNA insertion, targeted deletion, and complementation showed ZtGT2 is the causal gene underlying loss of hyphal growth on solid surfaces and loss of virulence. 2. ZtGT2 expression is induced during hyphal growth and the protein is associated with the cell wall fraction. 3. ΔZtGT2 mutants display abnormal outer wall ultrastructure but retain adhesion and many early surface-responsive transcriptional programs, indicating a specific role in outer wall polysaccharide function rather than general surface sensing.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant identification by TAIL-PCR; targeted gene deletion; genetic complementation; plant infection assays; qRT-PCR; western blot with peptide antibody; TEM; RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Represents a potential antifungal target for broad disease control because orthologues are conserved in many filamentous fungal pathogens and are required for virulence-related hyphal growth on plant surfaces

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: fungal extracellular/outer cell wall polysaccharide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: outer cell wall / extracellular matrix polysaccharide module
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely synthesizes or modifies an unknown extracellular or outer cell wall polysaccharide required for hyphal growth on solid surfaces
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms or modifies the functional outer wall/extracellular polysaccharide required for contact-dependent hyphal extension
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: unknown extracellular or outer cell wall polysaccharide
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fungal outer cell wall / extracellular matrix
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports radial hyphal growth on solid surfaces and virulence on wheat ears
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: selection in liquid versus solid medium affected recovery of deletion mutants; growth on solid PDA; wheat ear infection
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for contact-dependent hyphal growth on solid surfaces; not established as biochemical rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal radial hyphal growth on agar and full virulence on wheat ears; homokaryotic deletion mutants are severely growth impaired on solid surfaces but still capable of invasive growth through cellophane
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion mutants difficult to recover on solid selection, suggesting strong selection against loss during filamentous growth
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Standard solid-medium selection yielded heterokaryons retaining both WT and deleted alleles; liquid-medium selection enabled recovery of homokaryotic null mutants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FgGT2 is a likely 1:1 orthologue of ZtGT2. 2. Homokaryotic ΔFgGT2 mutants obtained via liquid selection are severely impaired in surface hyphal growth and nonpathogenic on wheat ears. 3. Invasive penetration through cellophane is retained, indicating FgGT2 is specifically critical for subsequent radial growth on solid surfaces rather than all invasive growth.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: split-marker gene deletion; diagnostic PCR; altered transformation/selection protocol; agar growth assays; wheat ear infection assays; cellophane penetration assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports GT2 orthologues as conserved antifungal targets across diverse wheat pathogens for disease control strategies
